industries.name: Chemical Manufacturing (Chlor-Alkali & Soda Ash)
uses: Chlor-alkali electrolysis of sodium chloride (brine) produces chlorine gas and sodium hydroxide (caustic soda, NaOH) simultaneously — the two most important commodity chemicals in the world by production volume
Solvay process converts sodium chloride and limestone into sodium carbonate (soda ash, Na₂CO₃) — essential feedstock for flat glass, container glass, detergents, and paper
Sodium hydroxide used to manufacture pulp and paper (kraft process), aluminum (Bayer process), soaps, textiles, and as a pH regulator across dozens of industrial processes
Sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) produced from chlorine and caustic soda for water treatment, bleaching, and household disinfectants
Sodium sulfate (Na₂SO₄) byproduct used in detergent powders and kraft paper pulping

companies.name: Solvay
companies.use: Invented the Solvay process in 1863; still the world's largest soda ash producer. Operates plants across Europe, North America, and Asia supplying glass manufacturers, detergent producers, and chemical companies.

companies.name: Tronox / Ciner Group
companies.use: Ciner operates natural trona-based soda ash mines in Wyoming (cheapest production route); Tronox supplies synthetic soda ash globally. Together represent a major share of world soda ash supply.

companies.name: Olin Corporation
companies.use: Largest US chlorine and caustic soda producer via chlor-alkali electrolysis. Supplies chlorine to PVC manufacturers, water treatment, and pulp/paper industries.

companies.name: Westlake Corporation
companies.use: Major integrated chlor-alkali and PVC producer. Electrolyzes sodium chloride brine to produce chlorine and caustic soda for downstream PVC and chemical manufacturing.

companies.name: BASF
companies.use: Major consumer and producer of sodium-based chemicals including sodium hydroxide, sodium methylate, and sodium cyanide at its Verbund integrated chemical sites.

industries.name: Food Processing & Agriculture
uses: Sodium chloride (table salt) as the world's oldest and most widely used food preservative — inhibits microbial growth by reducing water activity in meats, fish, cheeses, and fermented foods
Sodium bicarbonate (baking soda, NaHCO₃) as a leavening agent in baked goods — reacts with acids to release CO₂ and create rise in breads, cakes, and crackers
Sodium nitrate and nitrite (NaNO₃, NaNO₂) for curing and color preservation in processed meats including bacon, ham, hot dogs, and deli meats
Monosodium glutamate (MSG) as umami flavor enhancer in processed foods, soups, sauces, and snack seasonings globally
Sodium phosphates as emulsifiers, pH buffers, and moisture retention agents in processed cheese, deli meats, and seafood products

companies.name: Morton Salt (K+S Group)
companies.use: Leading US producer of food-grade, water-softening, and de-icing salt. Mines rock salt and produces evaporated salt for food processing, agriculture, and water treatment industries.

companies.name: Cargill Salt
companies.use: Major salt producer supplying food manufacturers, agriculture, and chemical industries. Operates solar evaporation facilities, rock salt mines, and vacuum evaporation plants across North America.

companies.name: Ajinomoto
companies.use: World's largest producer of monosodium glutamate (MSG). Ferments sugar feedstocks to produce glutamic acid, then neutralizes with sodium hydroxide to form MSG for food manufacturers globally.

companies.name: Compass Minerals
companies.use: Operates the Goderich mine in Ontario — the world's largest underground salt mine. Produces rock salt for food processing, water conditioning, and highway de-icing applications.

companies.name: Kerry Group
companies.use: Major food ingredients company using sodium-based preservatives, emulsifiers, and flavor enhancers across its portfolio of processed food ingredients supplied to global food manufacturers.

industries.name: Metallurgy & Metal Reduction
uses: Metallic sodium used as reducing agent to produce titanium metal via the Hunter process (Na reduces TiCl₄ to Ti metal) — an alternative to the dominant Kroll process
Sodium used in production of tetraethyllead (now phased out) and in synthesis of organosodium reagents for specialty chemical manufacturing
Sodium-sulfur (NaS) batteries for grid-scale energy storage use molten sodium as the anode material operating at ~300°C
Sodium amalgam (sodium-mercury alloy) historically used in chlor-alkali mercury cell electrolysis — now largely replaced by membrane cell technology
Liquid sodium metal used as coolant in fast breeder nuclear reactors due to excellent heat transfer properties and low neutron absorption cross-section

companies.name: TOHO Titanium
companies.use: Japanese titanium metal producer using sodium reduction (Hunter process) alongside Kroll process. Supplies aerospace and industrial titanium to manufacturers including Boeing, Airbus, and automotive OEMs.

companies.name: NGK Insulators
companies.use: World's dominant manufacturer of sodium-sulfur (NaS) grid-scale battery systems. Uses molten sodium metal as anode in high-temperature stationary energy storage installations at utilities worldwide.

companies.name: DuPont (Invista)
companies.use: Uses sodium in specialty chemical synthesis processes including production of nylon intermediates and organosodium reagents at integrated chemical manufacturing sites.

industries.name: Water Treatment & Municipal Services
uses: Sodium hypochlorite (liquid bleach) as primary disinfectant for municipal drinking water treatment — generated on-site or supplied in bulk to water utilities
Sodium chloride in ion-exchange water softeners — regenerates resin beds by displacing calcium and magnesium ions that cause hardness
Sodium aluminate (NaAlO₂) as coagulant aid in water clarification alongside aluminum sulfate (alum)
Sodium fluoride and sodium fluorosilicate added to municipal water supplies for dental caries prevention (water fluoridation programs)
Sodium hydroxide for pH adjustment in drinking water treatment to prevent pipe corrosion and optimize disinfection efficiency

companies.name: Veolia Water Technologies
companies.use: World's largest water treatment company. Operates municipal water and wastewater treatment plants globally using sodium hypochlorite, sodium hydroxide, and sodium-based coagulants at industrial scale.

companies.name: Evoqua Water Technologies
companies.use: Major supplier of on-site sodium hypochlorite generation systems (OSEC) for municipalities, eliminating the need to transport hazardous chlorine gas to water treatment facilities.

companies.name: Ecolab
companies.use: Global water treatment and hygiene company. Supplies sodium-based treatment chemicals, sodium hypochlorite, and sodium hydroxide to industrial water systems, food processing plants, and hospitality.

industries.name: Highway De-icing & Infrastructure
uses: Rock salt (sodium chloride) spread on roads and highways to lower freezing point of water — effective to approximately -9°C (15°F) without additives
Liquid brine pre-treatment (23% NaCl solution) applied before snowfall to prevent ice bonding to road surface — more effective and uses less salt than post-storm spreading
Sodium acetate and sodium formate used as airport runway de-icers where chloride corrosion to aircraft and concrete is a concern
Salt brine injection into sand/grit mixtures for anti-skid compounds on bridges, intersections, and steep grades

companies.name: Morton Salt (K+S Group)
companies.use: Dominant US highway de-icing salt supplier. Produces road salt from Weeks Island, Louisiana mine and Great Lakes rock salt operations. Supplies state DOTs and municipalities across the northern US.

companies.name: Cargill Deicing Technology
companies.use: Major road salt and liquid brine supplier to US state departments of transportation. Operates the Cleveland salt mine and Lansing, Michigan mine. Also markets ClearLane enhanced deicer.

companies.name: Compass Minerals
companies.use: Produces road salt from the world's largest underground salt mine in Goderich, Ontario. Major supplier to US and Canadian highway authorities for winter road maintenance.
